data.title: Methanex Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30
data.description:
• Methanex press release (MEOH): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30.
  • Our average realized price in the first quarter was $351 per tonne compared to $331 per tonne in the fourth quarter of 2025. Based on our April and May posted prices, we expect that our average realized price range will be approximately $500 to $525 per tonne for these two months.
  •

data.title: Bausch Health rises on Q1 double beat, 2026 guidance
data.description:
• Bausch Health Companies (BHC) shares are higher in post-market trading Wednesday after beating on both lines and issuing a 2026 revenue guidance range that includes the consensus.
  • The company is projecting full-year revenue of $10.67B-$10.92B. The consensus is $10.68B.
  • Q1 non-GAAP EPS of $0.78 compares to $0.59 in the year-ago period.
  • Bausch Health was helped in the period by 9% YoY revenue growth to ~$2.5B.
  • Cash and cash equivalents at the end of the quarter (March 31) were ~$1.3B, roughly the same as on Dec. 31, 2025.

data.title: Arch signals ~$250M 2026 net premium headwind as it tightens underwriting in a more competitive market
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Arch Capital Group Ltd. (ACGL) Q1 2026

Management view

  • "We delivered a strong quarter, reflecting both attractive underwriting margin and the disciplined execution of our underwriting and capital management strategies." (CEO & Director Nicolas Alain Papadopoulo)

  • "Today's market is clearly more competitive than in recent years." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo) "That said, rates and terms and conditions in aggregate still support strong returns." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo)

  • "Top line growth in the segment was essentially flat in the quarter, reflecting our focus on profitability over volume as competitive pressures increase." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo) "We also chose not to renew certain program business acquired in the middle market commercial transaction that did not align with our risk appetite or meet our profitability requirements." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo)

  • "Earlier this month, our team successfully completed the data and system migration of the acquired businesses from Allianz to Arch own systems." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo) "The ability to complete this effort in just 18 months...represents a strong use case for artificial intelligence in accelerating systems and platform transformation." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo)

  • "Strong industry results over the past few years have attracted significant new capacity from traditional markets and third party capital, resulting in a broadly competitive environment." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo) "This additional supply continues to put downward pressure on property catastrophe and short-term rates." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo)

  • "The mortgage segment delivered another strong quarter with $221 million of underwriting income to go along with $266 million of net premiums written." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo) "Competition remains disciplined, and we continue to pursue growth through innovation and new product introductions across our global footprint." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo)

  • "In the first quarter, we repurchased $783 million worth of Arch common stock while still increasing book value per share by 1.7%." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo) "The Board's recent $3 billion increase to our share repurchase authorization underscores this approach to capital allocation." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo)

  • "Last night, we reported our first quarter results with after-tax operating income of $2.50 per share." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer François Morin) "Our underwriting income included $200 million of favorable prior year development on a pretax basis in the first quarter." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Morin)

Outlook

  • "Today's market is clearly more competitive than in recent years." (CEO & Director Nicolas Alain Papadopoulo) "Capturing those returns requires the ability and willingness to actively manage the portfolio across and within lines of business." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo)

  • "These nonrenewals are expected to reduce net premium written by approximately $250 million throughout 2026." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo)

  • "We would expect our operating expense ratio to revert back to a level closer to historical levels during the second half of the year." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer François Morin)

  • "We don't -- as we always said, we don't have a crystal ball, but for the 6/1, I think we really expect the market to remain competitive and to adjust our underwriting stand based on the actual rate decrease that we will see at that time." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo)

Financial results

  • "After-tax operating income for the quarter was $901 million or $2.50 per share." (CEO & Director Nicolas Alain Papadopoulo) "Book value per share grew by 1.7% in the quarter." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer François Morin)

  • "Current year catastrophe losses were $174 million, net of reinsurance and reinstatement premiums and were mainly the result of winter storms in the U.S. and the Iran conflict." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Morin) "All in, these losses were slightly lower than our seasonally adjusted expectations for natural catastrophes." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Morin)

  • "The Reinsurance Segment delivered an excellent $441 million of underwriting income in the quarter." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo) "Our focused and disciplined underwriting led to the reinsurance group's 76% combined ratio, marking the fourth straight quarter of sub 80% combined ratios." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo)

  • "On the investment front, we earned a combined $568 million from net investment income and income from funds accounted using the equity method or $1.57 per share pretax." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Morin) "Cash flow from operations remained positive at $1.2 billion for the quarter." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Morin)

  • "Our effective tax rate on pretax operating income was 14.8%." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Morin) "It was slightly below the 16% to 18% previously guided range, mostly due to a 1.7% benefit from discrete items." (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Morin)

Q&A

  • Elyse Greenspan, Wells Fargo Securities, LLC: "I was just hoping to get some of your expectations for the midyear renewals." CEO & Director Papadopoulo: "We really expect the market to remain competitive and to adjust our underwriting stand based on the actual rate decrease that we will see at that time."

  • David Motemaden, Evercore ISI: "Just to get an update on the insurance book, where we stand just on rate versus trend." CEO & Director Papadopoulo: "For North America, I think we're seeing rate slightly below trend...If you go to international...overall, a low 1-digit rate decrease over trend overall."

  • Cave Montazeri, Deutsche Bank AG: "Is there any reason why you couldn't pay out 100% of income...what held you back from doing more this quarter?" Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Morin: "In terms of like could we buy back 100% of our income for the year? We could."

  • Joshua Shanker, BofA Securities: "Do you expect Arch's and the industry's loss ratios to begin to deteriorate from here?" CEO & Director Papadopoulo: "We are confident in our ability to manage the cycle...we think that we see rates just below trend. So that would support the thesis that margins are sustainable at least for the near future."

  • Ryan Tunis, Cantor Fitzgerald & Co.: "To what extent are you looking at managing the OpEx side of things as a potential source of boosting margins?" CEO & Director Papadopoulo: "I think the answer is yes. That's something that's in our mind."

  • Yaron Kinar, Mizuho Securities USA LLC: "Can you break them out for us for insurance and reinsurance" (on Iran-related losses). Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Morin: "Well, we don't break out -- I mean, we report everything as part of cats."

Sentiment analysis

  • "I don't want to sound greedy" and repeated capital return framing (Deutsche Bank; multiple analysts) pointed to slightly negative-to-pressing analyst tone on capital deployment and softening pricing, while management repeatedly used conditional language such as "we'll react to what's in front of us" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer François Morin).

  • Management tone was slightly positive in prepared remarks ("We delivered a strong quarter" (CEO & Director Nicolas Alain Papadopoulo)) and more measured in Q&A around pricing and renewals ("we don't have a crystal ball" (CEO & Director Papadopoulo)).

  • Versus last quarter’s "measured optimism" framing (CEO & Director Papadopoulo, Q4 2025), the current quarter used more direct language on competition ("Today's market is clearly more competitive" (CEO & Director Papadopoulo)).

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • "Today's market is clearly more competitive than in recent years." (CEO & Director Nicolas Alain Papadopoulo, Q1 2026) compared with "We head into 2026 with measured optimism" and "competition is increasing in several lines of business" (CEO & Director Papadopoulo, Q4 2025).

  • Capital return messaging remained consistent across quarters, but Q1 2026 added a larger authorization detail: "The Board's recent $3 billion increase to our share repurchase authorization underscores this approach to capital allocation." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo, Q1 2026) compared with "we don't set a target" and "you should expect us to be pretty active" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer François Morin, Q4 2025).

  • Operational execution emphasis shifted from Q4’s expense/tax-credit framework to Q1’s integration milestone: "successfully completed the data and system migration" (CEO & Director Papadopoulo, Q1 2026) while Q4 guidance language highlighted "a full year 2026 operating expense ratio between 3.9% and 4.5%" (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Morin, Q4 2025).

Risks and concerns

  • "Strong industry results...have attracted significant new capacity...resulting in a broadly competitive environment." (CEO & Director Nicolas Alain Papadopoulo) "This additional supply continues to put downward pressure on property catastrophe and short-term rates." (CEO & Director Papadopoulo)

  • "We do expect more losses to come through in the second quarter" (on Iran conflict exposure). (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer François Morin)

  • "We see it more as an increased systemic risk" (on cyber and AI). (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Morin)

Final takeaway

Arch management described Q1 2026 as a "strong quarter" supported by underwriting margin and capital management, while repeatedly characterizing market conditions as "more competitive"—particularly in property catastrophe and other short-tail lines—paired with active portfolio rotation and program nonrenewals expected to reduce net premium written by approximately $250 million throughout 2026. Management highlighted continued share repurchases backed by a $3 billion authorization increase, a completed middle market systems migration described as an AI-enabled acceleration, and risk framing that included competitive pricing pressure, expected additional Iran-conflict-related losses in Q2, and heightened cyber systemic risk considerations.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Broadstone Net Lease FFO of $0.40 beats by $0.01, revenue of $121.4M beats by $2.68M
data.description:
• Broadstone Net Lease press release (BNL): Q1 FFO of $0.40 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $121.4M (+11.7% Y/Y) beats by $2.68M.

  • For 2026, BNL expects to report AFFO of $1.53 to $1.57 per diluted share vs $1.59 consensus, which remains unchanged.

    The guidance is based on the following key assumptions:

    1. investments in real estate properties between $500 million and $625 million;
    2. dispositions of real estate properties between $75 million and $100 million; and
    3. total core general and administrative expenses between $30 million and $31 million.

data.title: Employers Holdings Non-GAAP EPS of $0.53 beats by $0.02
data.description:
• Employers Holdings press release (EIG): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.53 beats by $0.02.
  • Gross premiums written of $180.8 million, versus $212.1 million;
  • Net premiums earned of $180.9 million, versus $183.0 million;
  • Loss and LAE ratio increased to 71.4% from 66.0%;
  • Commission expense ratio increased to 13.1% from 12.6%;
  • Underwriting expense ratio improved to 22.6% from 23.4%;
  • GAAP combined ratio of 107.1% (107.7% excluding the LPT), versus 102.0% (102.8% excluding the LPT);
  • Net investment income of $28.3 million, versus $32.1 million;
  • Net realized and unrealized losses on investments of $1.7 million, versus $12.8 million;
  • Policies in-force of 130,321, versus 133,121;
  • Book value per share including the Deferred Gain of $51.26, an increase of 8.9%, and Adjusted book value per share of $51.75, up 4.5% (both growth rates include dividends declared); and
